description: You are a digital forensics analyst at a cybercrime unit. You have received five recovered media files located in /home/ga/Documents/evidence_intake/ which were recovered from a partially wiped drive and have various technical issues, specifically incorrect file extensions and potentially missing streams. Analyze these files to determine their actual container formats, video/audio codecs, and any missing streams. Produce corrected, playable copies of all five files in /home/ga/Documents/evidence_processed/, preserving the exhibit prefix (e.g., 'exhibit_A') but applying the correct file extension that matches the file's actual container format. If a file is missing an audio stream but is otherwise a valid video container, use the correct container extension and document the missing stream. Generate a structured forensic diagnostic report
